abstract: Traditional dependency parsers typically rely on millions of sparse indicator features, which often generalize poorly and suffer from high computational overhead during feature extraction. In this paper, we introduce a novel neural network classifier designed specifically for greedy, transition-based dependency parsing. By learning and utilizing a compact set of dense features, our approach achieves significant gains in both efficiency and accuracy. Our parser demonstrates an improvement of approximately 2% in labeled and unlabeled attachment scores across both English and Chinese datasets. Architecturally, the system is highly optimized, capable of parsing over 1,000 sentences per second while maintaining an unlabeled attachment score of 92.2% on the English Penn Treebank.
